chunks.text: In this video, we will see how images are created in HTML, lists are created, and tables are created. So, please like the video. I know very few people like videos on YouTube. Starting the fifth video of the Sigma Web Development course, let's roll the intro. So guys, I have come to my computer screen, as you can see. Now, first of all, I want to tell you that you should bookmark github.com/codewithharry. In fact, bookmark this repository so that you get all the code. Secondly, you can bookmark the playlist by clicking here. Click here on the star and click here to save it. This playlist will be yours.

chunks.topic: HTML Image Tag and Attributes (src, alt, width, height)
chunks.text: Now, the first thing I will tell you here is about images, what an image is inside HTML. So what we will do here is quickly create a folder, named video 5, new folder, and here video 5. You must know this. Right-clicking and opening with code, I will do it very fast this time, because I have already told you many times. If you don't want to see this welcome page repeatedly, then uncheck 'show welcome page on startup' here. And you won't see this repeatedly. But what do we have to do? We will create index.html. We will zoom in a bit using the control mouse wheel. We will toggle screencast mode so that you know which button I am pressing and when, and why. Now I will bring the Emmet abbreviation here by typing an exclamation mark. And here I will do view word wrap. It takes a lot of effort, you can see here that your word wrap is now off, okay. So now what will happen here is that the horizontal scroll bar will not appear, okay. Now what I will do is write images here. First, I will write images here. And I will simply bring an image here. Now you can get an image from the internet. I will write here, let's say we want to bring an image of a train. Okay, train images. Because we have run a lot of trains in this course. So we will bring train images, why would we bring any other image? I will do save image as. And here, inside the Sigma Web Development course, in the video number 5 folder. This is a CMS file. In fact, I'll do one thing, I'll cancel this. I'll take a snip of it, because this is a CMS extension, I want it in PNG. So I pressed Windows, Shift, and S here. It's a shortcut for the snipping tool, if you don't know. So now, through this Sigma Web Development course, you know that if you press Windows, Shift, and S, your snipping tool opens in Windows. Who all didn't know this, tell me honestly in the comment section, okay. Now what we will do here, I had already done Windows Shift S, and then I got caught up in talking. So click here and save it here, click on this floppy disk save option here. And go to downloads, then to Sigma Web Development course, and save it in video 5 as image, okay, saved as image. Now, in HTML, there is an IMG tag. As soon as you type IMG, both SRC and ALT are its required attributes. In fact, even if you don't write ALT, it will work, but it's recommended. I will tell you what happens with these two attributes. First, if you write image.png inside SRC, you can see this image is here, so image.png. And in ALT, I will put train image, okay. Now if I show you this by previewing it in the browser. So look, it looks something like this, this is our page and the train image is visible here. What happened by doing this, where did the ALT we wrote go? You might ask, I'll tell you where the content we put inside ALT went. So, the thing we put inside ALT, we didn't see it yet. Suppose for some reason the server is unable to show you the image, okay. Then what will happen? Suppose I accidentally wrote image 2. Now, there is no file named image2.png here. So will there be an error, what will happen, I'll show you what will happen. So here, the ALT will be displayed, and an error image like this will be displayed. Which will tell you that, hey, the image you've put, fix it, you've messed something up, or the image doesn't even exist on the server URL you provided, okay. Such things are shown like this, and the ALT is visible there. So the user gets to know what the purpose of this image is, okay. And now if I come back here, you can add ALT to the image like this. If I don't even put ALT, will anything happen? Oh, if I don't put ALT, then the ALT text that was appearing won't appear, and nothing else will happen. So you wouldn't want the user to see this image when there's an error on your server. You would want the scenario to be such that the user sees something like this, 'train image' written, like this, okay. I hope you understood this. So image is very straightforward. It has two more attributes that are very important, one is width, which is width in pixels. If I set width equal to 23, then you can see. In fact, I will fix the image first, I accidentally wrote image 2. It's image.png, so here you can see I took 23 pixels, which is very small. So I will make it 230 pixels. You don't need to write 'px' in this. In CSS, when we write width, we will see there that we need to write 'px', here we just write the number inside double quotes for width, as you can see, okay. So if I bring it here, you see that we got a small image with width. If I also give it a height, suppose I give it a height. I say, keep its height at 230 as well, then its aspect ratio will get distorted. So it will stretch a bit, because it made its width 230 and height 230, as you can see. Now if you want its height to be 230 and width to adjust automatically, then just use the height attribute. You remove the width one here. So its height will be 230 pixels, and it will take as much width as it can, it will adjust automatically, you see, it adjusted automatically in width and took this in height. Now, width and height can also be adjusted through CSS. And in the CSS section, we will see why we should always set this width and height; we should always set at least one of width or height. Such that when our page is loading, the image inside our page doesn't suddenly appear on the page. I will tell you this in upcoming videos. But for now, understand that these width and height attributes, I have heard many people say that they are deprecated and should not be used now. But I say they should be used for SEO purposes. Why they should be used, I will tell you in upcoming videos, where I will tell you about Core Web Vitals and SEO. For now, understand that width gives width to the image, height gives height to the image. If you apply one of them, the other one automatically gets set. And these values are in pixels and you don't have to write px or pixel, you just have to write this number and that's it. This is one attribute, height, and there's an SRC attribute, an ALT attribute. In this, you can also use the style attribute. In fact, you can use the style attribute with almost all tags. So this was about images.

chunks.topic: HTML Tables (table, tr, th, td, rowspan, colspan, caption, thead, tbody, tfoot)
chunks.text: Now, let's quickly talk about tables in HTML. Inside HTML, the table tag is very useful. What happens with the table tag, I'll tell you. With the table tag, you get to see a table. Now I will copy this table from here, and when I copy this table from here, you will see that my page will look something like this. So this HTML table, where is my preview page, here it is, here you see name, Harry, age, 100, okay. Now if I add many more things here, suppose I add something like this. Suppose I add name. And here I add, let us say, designation, Harry's designation, suppose I add programmer. And let's say I want to add more things. I will use Alt Shift bottom arrow key, I will use Alt Shift bottom arrow key. And here, let's say I want to write favorite language and I want to write, suppose, JavaScript, okay. And now you can see here that this table will be created like this: name, destination, favorite language, okay. So what is this, how does it work, I'll tell you. So here, you see that we used a table tag, inside the table tag there is a TR tag, which is a table row. What all will come inside this row, you will see. Now, if you want to use this as a table heading, you say that this is my table row, meaning this row, this row, where name, destination, and your favorite language are written. What all will come inside this row and what will those things be? Here you can see that it will be a heading. So that's why I will use TH, TH means table heading. And we will write that only once, so inside the first TR, we will write TH. Once we have put the heading above, then we will put the data. So we will put TD inside the table row, which stands for table data. And here, as many such rows you put inside TR, your rows will keep getting added. So here, suppose I write Sam, and he is also a programmer, and suppose Java is his favorite language. So these rows will keep getting added below the table, you can see here that these rows are getting added, getting added, getting added. With CSS, we will see how to give it a border, how to style it. But by default, it looks like this. And yes, I know it's ugly, but for now, just understand that the table works something like this. Now there are rowspan and colspan attributes. Now you see here that rowspan means how many rows your TD will span. For example. Let us say I write here, I want Sam and programmer, earlier they were taking one cell each, Sam was taking one cell, programmer was taking one cell. I want to write Sam in two cells. So I would want not to write programmer, just Sam. And I want to write here that colspan is equal to 2. So colspan equal to 2 means that Sam will now take up two columns. What does this mean, Sam will now take up two columns. Meaning Sam will look something like this, I'll show you. Something like this, so it took up the entire column, okay. So colspan means how many columns will be occupied. By default, one cell will occupy only one column, but sometimes one cell will occupy two columns. To demonstrate this, I will explain it to you using Excel. In Excel, understand that this is our HTML table. Sometimes I will want one data to span two columns. So here we use merge cell and here suppose I write Sam. In HTML, this is done using colspan. Sometimes we also want to merge vertically. Meaning, suppose I want to merge these two cells. There we use rowspan. And with whom will we use rowspan, we will obviously use rowspan with TD, okay. So with TD, we were using colspan as well as rowspan. If we use rowspan, it will span two rows like this, okay. So I hope you understood this, and when we create tables, create HTML projects, then you will understand it even better. But I will quickly show you what rowspan is here. So if I write rowspan equal to 2 here and then write another TR here. Then you will see that my table looks something like this. Now, to better explain this, I will put a CSS style here. What I will do here is add a stylesheet, and I am going to name this stylesheet style.css. So in style.css, and you don't need to understand CSS at all right now. Let me tell you already. Inside TD, I will write border 2 pixel solid black. And I will tell you all how this works, but for now, understand that I have put this to explain it to you. So I said that this row will have Harry, programmer, and JavaScript. Then I said that this Sam, what will be its rowspan? Where did I give rowspan two? First, I said that my Java will have rowspan 2. So how many rows will Java take, obviously two, okay. So here Java will take two rows. So here you can see Java took two rows. So when Java took two rows, it means I don't need to write Java in the next one, because its row is already occupied, so I won't write Java in the next one, okay. So you see, I won't write Java here. I made its colspan 2. I made its rowspan 2. So what happened by doing this, you can see it was Harry, programmer, JavaScript. Then I said that this Sam, make its colspan 2, meaning it will occupy two columns. This Sam will also occupy two columns. And this Java will occupy two rows. So you can see here exactly that happened, it occupied two rows, it occupied two columns. So this is how rowspan and colspan can be used inside HTML. Inside an HTML table, we can also add captions. And to add captions, we can use the caption tag like this. So I'll show you that if we use caption inside the table, student details, here I'll say employee details. Then your page will look something like this, you can see here that the employee details caption will be added here. Now, with CSS, this can also be changed, its font, its font size, but for now, by default, it looks something like this, the caption comes at the top, employee details will appear like this to you. Along with that, you can also give header and footer, you can give T head and T foot here. For example, if I put some content inside T head and T foot, like this, if I put header here and suppose I put footer here. Then you will see T head and T foot like this, you see header here, footer here. Now I will tell you about one more tag here, which is the BR tag, which adds a line break. So after the image, there will be a line break here, you can see everything came down. So this is the header, and after this, I will obviously add the footer below. I will not add it here. So I will add everything after my table, at the end. So this is my table footer. Now I can change this with CSS. Now here, I can group the content of my header and footer with T head and T foot like this. So what will be the header of my table, this TH that I have written here, I can group it, I can put it inside T head. Along with that, the content inside the table will come inside T body. So what will come inside T body, my table content will come something like this, as you can see. And after this, if I write T foot here and I want to, at the end, suppose I want to put some data, then I will put another TR, as you can see. I will right-click and format the document, so it will be formatted a bit better. Suppose I want to put this kind of TR and I want to group it in T foot. So this is just a way to group. Meaning I put some TRs inside T foot, I put my main body inside T body, and I put my table header inside T head. And obviously, nothing will change with this, I have only done grouping. Here, look, our, I'll do one thing, I'll write footer here, so that you know this is the footer. I'll write footer here. And you can see here that our last row is the foot. Now you will say, what was the benefit of doing this? We have only done grouping so that we can target it further with CSS. So you will use this very rarely, but you can do grouping like this. Your entire table, head content will come here, foot content will come here. And body content actually comes in between the head and foot, but you can write it here in any way. But ideally, head, body, and then foot comes. Okay, I hope you understood this. So that's all about HTML tables, you don't need to know more than this right now.

chunks.video_title: Image, Lists, and Tables in HTML
chunks.topic: HTML Lists (Ordered, Unordered, Definition) and Editor Recommendations
chunks.text: And now we will talk about lists here. Lists are very important in HTML, because we will use lists a lot. There are three types of lists in HTML: we have an ordered list, an unordered list, and a definition list. The most commonly used are unordered and ordered lists. First, let's look at the unordered list. Whenever we want to have unordered data. Like if I write UL here. So as soon as I write UL here, it means unordered list. Inside it, I can write LI. And I can write Harry. And after this, I can write Rohan, okay. And after this, I can write Shubham, okay. So here, when I write Harry, Rohan, Shubham, and I do this UL, it will appear like this. This is an unordered list. Look, Harry, Rohan, Shubham appeared here. They have no order. 1, 2, 3, 4 did not appear in it. But if I want it to be ordered, then I can write the same thing with the help of OL. So if I make its opening and closing tag OL and keep other things the same. Then it will become an ordered list, okay. So here it will have 1, 2, 3. You can make this 1, 2, 3 into ABC, you can also make it capital ABC, you can also make it Roman numbers. You can also do it with the help of CSS, and there are other ways to do it. You can see here in this image that when LI was done inside UL, it appeared like this. In this image, you can see here that when LI was done inside OL, it came as 1, 2, 3. This became an example of an ordered list. And this became an example of a definition list. Definition list is used very rarely. Its preview looks something like this. Here, first I put a heading, then after that I wrote DL. DL means definition list. After this, DT and then DD. So here there is a definition term, and then there is the definition of its definition. So it comes something like this. And you can also change it with CSS. It's used very rarely, but you should know about it for interviews. Interviewers sometimes ask what a definition list is. So this kind of code is previewed like this. I am also not writing it out for the sake of time, but try it out, okay. And here, first we will look at the unordered list. I have told you about unordered lists, it's very simple and straightforward. You can customize its bullet points using the type attribute. For example, if I make type equal to square here. I say here type is equal to and suppose I write square here, okay. So I wrote type equal to square inside UL, so look, it has appeared like a square. Similarly, here inside UL, I can make its type circle, square, or disc. Disc is by default, meaning the circle that appears is visible by default, okay. I can make it square, I can also make it circle, okay. Along with that, you can add any number of other list items inside its list items. Now let's look at the ordered list a bit more closely. In an ordered list, if you write LI here, it will appear as 1, 2, 3. But you can use type equal to I. When you use type equal to I, you will get uppercase Roman numbers. If you do type equal to small I, you will get lowercase Roman numbers. Type equal to 1 is the default, you don't need to do this. If you do small A, you will get alphabetical, and if you do capital A, you will get uppercase alphabetical. So let's try this once. So here I will make its type equal to capital A. Now look, VS Code is giving us examples, suggestions, and it becomes easy for us to see what its possible values are. So VS Code has many benefits. And many people are using old editors that were used a long time ago. If you know their correct plugins, and you are very used to them, then you will do it. VS Code is modern, switch to VS Code. Everyone uses VS Code, you will see all the big developers using VS Code. If you want to do coding on mobile, I recommend Replit. There's an app called Replit. And I would like to give a shoutout to replit.com here, because I use it quite a lot and I find it quite good. So you can also use replit.com on your mobile, if your computer's compute power is not that high, then you can use it. You get templates and stuff in it. We will cover more of this in upcoming videos. But for now, I will stick to VS Code. I hope you understood ordered list, unordered list, definition list here.
